meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: Space Force awards Viasat, SES $437 million for military satellite network
immediate.summary: The companies will build four small geostationary satellites for the Protected Tactical Satcom-Global program The post Space Force awards Viasat, SES $437 million for military satellite network appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: Space Force Awards $90 Million to Rocket Lab for Two GEO Satellites
immediate.summary: Space Force has awarded a $90 million contract to Rocket Lab for satellites to host payloads that tracks objects in geosynchronous orbit. The post Space Force Awards $90 Million to Rocket Lab for Two GEO Satellites appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: DARPA readies robotic deep-space repair satellite for 2026 launch
immediate.summary: DARPA has a plan to keep geosynchronous satellites going for years beyond their expiration date. The agency aims to test it with a mission launching as soon as this summer.

immediate.title: NASA to add missions to SpaceX commercial crew contract
immediate.summary: NASA plans to add more missions to SpaceX’s commercial crew contract, protecting the agency from the possibility that Boeing’s spacecraft is never certified for missions to the International Space Station. The post NASA to add missions to SpaceX commercial crew contract appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: Pentagon Finally Releases Footage Of Unidentified Object Shot Down By F-16 Over Lake Huron
immediate.summary: In 2023, a U.S. Air National Guard F-16CM shot down a UAP, or unidentified aerial phenomenon, over Lake Huron. Newly released footage appears to show that the object was likely a balloon. On Feb. 12, 2023, a U.S. Air National Guard F-16C shot down an unidentified object in the Canadian airspace above Lake Huron. At []

immediate.title: Space Force needs to prepare for an ‘in-person’ moon conflict with China, new report argues
immediate.summary: Guardians need a human spaceflight program for future lunar missions, Mitchell Institute says.

immediate.title: Nothing beats a beautiful sunset (from space) | Space photo of the day for May 26, 2026
immediate.summary: Nothing beats a beautiful sunset (from space) | Space photo of the day for May 26, 2026

immediate.title: Northern Norway is ready to launch. EU Space Regulation — and its new Arctic policy – is not.
immediate.summary: The European Commission is currently updating its Arctic policy, with a new policy statement expected this coming autumn. Unlike the latest policy from 2021, the update will place greater emphasis [] The post Northern Norway is ready to launch. EU Space Regulation — and its new Arctic policy – is not. appeared first on SpaceNews .

immediate.title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12: Don't miss these stunning photos from the launch of the most powerful Starship yet
immediate.summary: Starship V3 led to some stunning launch photography after it lifted off from Starbase on SpaceX's 12th flight of the rocket that aims to land astronauts on the moon.

immediate.title: China launches 3 astronauts, including 1st ever from Hong Kong, to Tiangong space station (video)
immediate.summary: China's Shenzhou 23 astronaut mission launched Sunday (May 24) to relieve an overdue crew on the Tiangong space station.

immediate.title: A-10 Revamped: Warthog Flying in Middle East with New Refueling Probe, EW Pod
immediate.summary: A-10 Thunderbolt II attack planes in the Middle East are flying with fresh modifications as the Air Force looks to make the plane more versatile amid America’s ongoing blockade of Iranian ports and a tenuous ceasefire in the U.S. air war against Iran. The post A-10 Revamped: Warthog Flying in Middle East with New Refueling Probe, EW Pod appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: Wilsbach: No Gap in USAF’s Close Air Support After A-10s Retire
immediate.summary: Air Force Chief of Staff Gen. Kenneth S. Wilsbach told lawmakers this week there will be no gap in the service’s close air support capabilities under its latest plan to retire the venerable A-10 Thunderbolt II at the start of the next decade. The post Wilsbach: No Gap in USAF’s Close Air Support After A-10s Retire appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

same_day.title: What is the blue-white star next to the moon tonight?
same_day.summary: Don't miss Spica greet the waxing moon in the southern sky on May 26

same_day.title: Scientists just found a supercharged supernova — powered up by a magnetic star corpse
same_day.summary: NASA's Fermi Gamma-ray spacecraft has observed a super-bright, supercharged supernova explosion powered up by the creation of a highly magnetic dead star, or magnetar.

same_day.title: Shenzhou-23 crew arrives at Tiangong as China maps path to 2030 lunar landing
same_day.summary: Three Chinese astronauts arrived at Tiangong space station Sunday, with one crewmember expected to become China’s first to stay in orbit for an entire year. The post Shenzhou-23 crew arrives at Tiangong as China maps path to 2030 lunar landing appeared first on SpaceNews .

same_day.title: 'There's mania': Strategists weigh in on looming SpaceX IPO
Lunar Strike offers a pessimistic, and worryingly realistic look at the future of space travel
This Week In Space podcast: Episode 211 — Oysters in Space
same_day.summary: On Episode 211 of This Week In Space, Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik talk with Jacob Scoccimerra of Monolith Space.

same_day.title: Blue Origin completes investigation into New Glenn launch failure
same_day.summary: Blue Origin has completed the investigation into the failure on the third flight of its New Glenn rocket, clearing launches of the vehicle to resume. The post Blue Origin completes investigation into New Glenn launch failure appeared first on SpaceNews .

same_day.title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3
same_day.summary: SpaceX launched the newest version of its Starship vehicle for the first time May 22, completing most of the test objectives planned for the suborbital flight. The post SpaceX launches first Starship V3 appeared first on SpaceNews .

same_day.title: SpaceX just launched Starship V3 — its most powerful megarocket yet — into space for the 1st time in spectacular Flight 12 test (video)
same_day.summary: SpaceX's Starship V3, the most powerful rocket ever built, just roared off its launch pad in a spectacular show of power and technology.

same_day.title: Call for Creatives: NASA Seeks Help Illuminating Mission Storytelling
same_day.summary: As NASA pushes the boundaries of exploration and innovation for the benefit of humanity, the agency is looking for partners to share mission stories covering Artemis Moon missions, nuclear propulsion, aeronautics, and more. NASA published an Announcement for Proposals on May 21 asking filmmakers, documentarians, songwriters, storytellers, poets, and others to submit proposals to partner []
